title: Empire C.C — Regenerate downloadable codebase ZIP export (frontend wiring)

requirements.summary: Render a fresh ZIP download link/button in the in-app chat when the backend (or assistant) posts a ZIP URL.
acceptanceCriteria: A single ZIP archive is produced containing frontend/, backend/, and all required root-level project configuration files (e.g., dfx.json, package manifests, build config) needed to build and deploy the same app.
A downloadable link/button is displayed to the user after generation, and clicking it downloads the ZIP successfully.
The ZIP contents match the current deployed version of the app (no missing recently-added files such as PWA assets and generated icons).

file_operations.description: Create a small chat message renderer that detects URLs in message content and renders them as accessible links, with special handling for .zip URLs (show a clear download action/button while preserving the original text).
Update message rendering to use ChatMessageContent so system messages that contain a ZIP URL display a dedicated download link/button (no changes under frontend/src/components/ui).

requirements.summary: Provide an English export README artifact in the frontend bundle and surface it in the UI so it can be included/validated during ZIP export regeneration.
acceptanceCriteria: The ZIP includes a top-level README.md written in English.
README.md includes step-by-step commands for installing dependencies and running the frontend locally.
README.md includes step-by-step commands for deploying the canister(s) with dfx.
README.md explains how users add/manage provider API keys via the existing in-app Key Vault UI flow (no external secret manager required).

file_operations.description: Add an English README artifact (static file) documenting dependency install, local run, dfx deploy, and how to configure provider keys via the existing in-app Key Vault flow; intended to be picked up by the ZIP export process as part of the codebase snapshot.

Add a minimal, non-intrusive card/section linking to the static export README (frontend/public/export/README.md) so users can open/verify the documentation from the app without changing overall navigation or UI/UX patterns.
